meta.note: 用于 Step2：以主体主图为参照图，生成同一场景的 3x3 多视图主体设计图。

input_slots.source_design_document: projects/aigc/<项目名>/5-设计/场景/2-设计/S###-<场景名>.md
input_slots.source_prompt: <直接引用场景设计文档 ## 5. 提示词设计 中的英文提示词>
input_slots.reference_main_image: projects/aigc/<项目名>/5-设计/场景/3-生成/<主体名称>-主图.<ext>

reference_policy.reference_main_image_role: continuity anchor for scene identity, material logic, spatial silhouette, lighting direction and color script

layout_generation_prompt.canvas_setup: Create a 16:9 widescreen scene multi-view design sheet on a clean dark neutral ground using a strict 3x3 nine-grid. Keep spacing, margins, and panel ratio uniform. Treat the sheet as a production design reference board for one coherent scene.
critical_requirements: The upstream scene design document's prompt section, especially `提示词设计`, is the primary source of truth. Use it directly for scene identity, era, style, spatial facts, architectural logic, material logic, lighting and narrative function.

Use the provided `reference_main_image` as a continuity anchor for the same scene subject; keep landmark identity, silhouette, material family, palette, lighting direction and spatial orientation consistent.
Do not redesign the scene. Do not add new architecture, new story elements, new people, creatures, silhouettes or human-shaped shadows unless the upstream design prompt explicitly requires them.
Render one coherent scene as a unified 3x3 panel sheet with strict cross-panel consistency.
Treat every panel as a view variant of the same space, not nine different scenes.
P1-P3: atmosphere plus iconography sequence: wide establishing view, medium spatial function view, close ritual or architectural detail.
P4/P5/P7/P8: structural and directional views that prove threshold design, circulation hierarchy, cosmology order, edge traversal, material joins, or sacred-light organization.
P6: low-angle up-shot emphasizing vertical authority, oppression or transcendence. P9: high-angle top-down shot revealing spatial order or cosmology logic.
Maintain one consistent spatial orientation across all panels; doors, thresholds, major landmarks and light direction must not flip randomly.
Include at least one scale-readable environmental cue such as steps, railing height, doorway size, furniture scale, floor tile rhythm or structural measurement logic, without adding people.
Panel labels, grid divisions and borders must remain clean and secondary; do not let labels, decorative UI or frame ornaments dominate the scene.

Reserve one fixed top-left identity badge area using matched roster text `<SCENE_ID>+<SCENE_NAME>`; if exact text rendering is unreliable, preserve the badge area and short ID cleanly for post-production text overlay.

panel_views.label: Atmosphere Wide
panel_views.description: Eye-level wide shot establishing full spatial envelope and dominant light logic.

panel_views.label: Atmosphere Medium
panel_views.description: Mid shot showing functional zoning, narrative center and the first clear era or culture cue.

panel_views.label: Ritual Detail
panel_views.description: Close architectural or material detail proving iconography, ceremonial logic or world-specific ornament.

panel_views.label: Threshold View A
panel_views.description: Directional view proving entrance, edge traversal, barrier or passage logic.

panel_views.label: Structural View
panel_views.description: View proving architectural support, material joins, scale and spatial hierarchy.

panel_views.label: Low-Angle Vertical Axis
panel_views.description: Low-angle up-shot emphasizing vertical authority, oppression or transcendence.

panel_views.label: Circulation View
panel_views.description: View proving movement path, ritual route, service path or cosmology linkage.

panel_views.label: Material / Cultural View
panel_views.description: View foregrounding material continuity, cultural threshold, ornament or era-coded detail.

panel_views.label: High-Angle Spatial Order
panel_views.description: High-angle top-down shot revealing spatial order, zoning or cosmology logic.

consistency.label: Lighting Continuity
consistency.description: Primary light source direction and intensity remain physically coherent in all panels.

consistency.label: Material Continuity
consistency.description: Material families and texture age state remain stable across panels.

consistency.label: Layout Discipline
consistency.description: Equal panel sizes, clean gutters, no overlap and no decorative frame noise.

negative_prompt.global: no people, no creatures, no silhouettes, no human-shaped shadows, no unrelated architecture, no scene redesign, no nine different locations, no random flipped orientation, no inconsistent era, no inconsistent materials, no unreadable clutter, no decorative UI dominance
negative_prompt.layout: no uneven grid, no overlapping panels, no broken gutters, no excessive labels, no ornamental frames dominating the scene
negative_prompt.reference_drift: do not copy artifacts from reference image, do not introduce accidental objects, do not change the canonical scene identity

prompt_assembly.prompt_text: Use `<source_prompt>` as the primary scene prompt. Using `<reference_main_image>` as a continuity reference, create a 16:9 3x3 multi-view production design sheet for `<subject_name>`. Follow all critical requirements and panel view modules. Preserve one coherent scene identity across all panels.
